preamble: Vowel lowering from a high front position to a low central/front position is a fundamental sound change that can occur through chain shifts, morphological patterns (ablaut), or long-term diachronic drift.

examples.note: Germanic ablaut pattern for past tense formation
The Great Vowel Shift lowered /i:/ to a diphthong starting with [a]

sources: The Great Vowel Shift in English
